description: Sure, the STEP surface quality isn't anything to write home about as it's full of gaps and lacks color, but I'm glad that it loaded anything at all and I was able to spin it around in Linux without resorting to using an online service!
summary: Actually loaded a STEP file from work!

description:
working good on feodra 42. grate to scee quicklly priview models. highlly recommended app for 3d artists, or gamedev.

just a suggustion add a functionality to scee poly cont of model and all small detils about model that will be grate.
A really cool tool for previewing 3D models.

This app shows potential as a simple 3d model viewer, but it is horribly laggy. I have no problem running CAD and other 3d rendering applications on my computer, but interacting with models in this software is painful.

I set this as the default app for all .stl and .stp files. That way I can just double click them from the file manager and get a quick look at all my models without having to open my slicer program (which takes a minute to start up) and import the model there.

My only complaint is that it does not appear to support 3mf files. This is a bummer because a lot of my own designs are FreeCAD files exported to 3mf. I hope 3mf support will be a feature in future versions.
Does exactly what it says. Lots of easily-configured options to toggle on the fly.
Perfect integration with gnome interface, just what I was looking for quick and fast visualization of files before going into any heavier software for editing.
Pretty much the ideal 3D model viewer, and fits like a glove into the GNOME design language. Great showcase of modern GTK. Love to see it!

I just moved from Windows to Fedora so to see this release at the same time feels very fortunate, and it follows the same pattern ive found with the GNOME platform in general - a precisely designed with the right balance of features that only appear when you need them, and it beats anything you can find on Windows.

My criticisms are pretty minor, the UI looks blurry when using fractional scaling and the performance could be better, but this only affects it's functionality in a minor way and I'm just grateful to have access to this, thanks.
